rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative oxysulfuration of alkenes with thiophenols and oxygen nucleophiles (water/alcohols/carboxylic acids) to give β-hydroxy sulfides, β-alkoxy sulfides, and thio-substituted γ-lactones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of thiophenol to aryl sulfur radical, and a second anodic oxidation of the resulting benzylic C-radical to a carbocation; cathode reduces protons to H2 and regenerates aryl sulfide anion.
electron_flow: At the anode, ArSH is oxidized by SET to ArS•, which adds to the alkene to give a benzylic radical B; B is further oxidized at the anode to benzylic cation C, which is trapped by the O-nucleophile (H2O, ROH, or carboxylate) to deliver the oxysulfuration product and a proton. At the cathode, protons are reduced to H2 while ArSH/ArS− is regenerated.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (toluylene, 49)
probe_reagents.observation: Trapping product 50 isolated in 72% yield, consistent with involvement of radical intermediates (ArS• adding to alkene).

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 4-chlorothiophenol (34)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 1.99 V and reduction peak at -1.33 V in MeCN; both fall within the applied cell potential, supporting thiophenol involvement in both anodic and cathodic processes.

notes: Lower yields for styrenes (secondary benzyl radical) vs α-methylstyrenes (tertiary benzyl radical) are taken as additional evidence for a radical pathway. The mechanism is presented as a paired electrolysis: anodic oxidation generates ArS• and the cation, while cathodic proton reduction releases H2 and regenerates ArSH via ArS−.
